summary: Matched a large block of fighter animation code in src/melee/ft/ftanim.c and supporting headers/types. The decomp.dev bot reported +8236 matched bytes, 30 newly matched functions in main/melee/ft/ftanim, one ftdynamics function improved to 100%, and ftAnim_80070308 improved to 80.95%. The PR also tightened animation-related data types, replacing placeholder lbMthp-style access with FigaTree/FigaTrack structures and propagating those types through ftdata, ftdynamics, lbmthp, lbanim, and fighter structs.

key_files.why: Added the bulk of matched ftAnim implementations, including joint-tree traversal, animation stepping/blending, frame queries, part animation setup, rate changes, callback wrapping, and costume texture animation handling.
Replaced placeholder include guard/style and expanded/corrected ftAnim prototypes with concrete Fighter, Fighter_GObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_Joint, FigaTree, and frame/rate parameter types.

Recovered animation-relevant fields: ftCommonData.x808 Vec3, named DObjList struct form, ftData nested x8 layout, FighterBone.xC as u8, Fighter.x590 as FigaTree*, costume TOBJ fields, ftData_80085FD4_ret flags, and ftDynamics.x10 as FigaTree***.
Moved FigaTrack and FigaTree into forward-declarable struct definitions and corrected FigaTree nodes/tracks to s8* and FigaTrack*.
Changed lbMthp_8001E8F8 to operate on FigaTree* and return tree->frames instead of reading placeholder x8.
Adjusted dynamic bone animation logic to pass FigaTree* values into ftCo_8009CB40/ftAnim_8006EED4 and matched ftCo_8009E4A8.
Split/identified ftAnim data symbols at 803C0670, 803C0690, and 803C06A8 as strings instead of one larger object.

what_changed: Implemented many previously unmatched ftAnim functions from 8006DBF4 through later 80070xxx helpers, with the bot listing 30 new ftanim matches.
Added static traversal stacks for HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_Joint with a fixed depth of 30 and the original OSReport/assert path for overflow.
Added joint traversal helpers/inlines for HSD_JObj next, parent, and child access and used them in tree-walking animation routines.
Implemented animation update paths that distinguish non-blended animation from blended x8AC_animSkeleton animation, including calls to HSD_AObjInitEndCallBack and HSD_AObjInvokeCallBacks.
Implemented frame/rate helpers such as ftAnim_8006F0FC, ftAnim_SetAnimRate, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ftAnim_8006F368, ftAnim_8006F3DC, and ftAnim_8006F484.
Updated ftAnim_8006EBA4 to call ftAnim_8006E9B4, ftAction_80073240, ftAnim_800707B0, and ftCo_800DB500 in sequence.
Corrected ftCo_800DB500 prototype from unknown return/params to void ftCo_800DB500(Fighter_GObj*).
Changed ftData_80085E50 return type from lbMthp_8001E8F8_t* to FigaTree*.
Changed ftdynamics dynamic-bone animation plumbing to pass FigaTree* and NULL rather than placeholder integer zero or Fighter_Part values.
Removed the old lbMthp_8001E8F8_t placeholder struct from lb/types.h after replacing its usage with FigaTree.
Changed ftParts_80075028 return type from u8 to int and refined several ftparts prototypes, including DObjList/HSD_TObj usage.
Updated GALE01 symbol metadata to mark ftAnim_803C0670, ftAnim_803C0690, and ftAnim_803C06A8 as separate string objects.
smart_moves: Recovered FigaTree/FigaTrack structure semantics and reused them across ftanim, ftdynamics, ftdata, lbanim, and lbmthp instead of preserving a one-off placeholder type.
Used exact lib/HSD callback bracketing around animation walks: HSD_AObjInitEndCallBack before traversal and HSD_AObjInvokeCallBacks after traversal.
Modeled tree traversal explicitly with small helper inlines for next, parent, and child, which likely helped expression ordering and repeated traversal code match.
Preserved original assert strings and line numbers in the joint-depth checks, which are often necessary for data/string references and codegen matching.
Kept nonmatching safety under #ifdef BUGFIX in ftAnim_8006F3DC, avoiding a behavior-changing unconditional return while documenting the undefined fallthrough case.
Replaced magic integer zero with NULL in pointer contexts, while also correcting the underlying parameter type to FigaTree* where evidence supported it.
Split a previously large ftAnim data symbol into three string symbols, improving data boundary accuracy for future symbol and rodata work.
decomp_lessons: When a placeholder struct is only used to reach a frame-count field, check whether it is actually an animation tree object; here lbMthp_8001E8F8_t became FigaTree and its x8 field became frames.
Matching animation systems often requires cross-file type recovery: ftanim matches depended on changes in ft/types.h, ftdata.h, ftdynamics.h, lbanim.h, lbmthp.h, and ftparts.h.
For HSD joint-tree walks, local helper accessors can be used to force the compiler into matching null-check and member-access structure without changing behavior.
Use forward declarations for shared structs such as FigaTree, FigaTrack, and DObjList to reduce placeholder includes and make headers composable.
Preserve original debug/assert behavior, including strings and line constants, because those details affect matching and data references.
Dynamic bone and animation blending code may store alternate skeletons or animation trees in fighter fields; correct field names/types can unlock several functions at once.
If a function can fall through in the retail binary, guard a modern safety return behind BUGFIX rather than silently changing matching behavior.
naming_conventions: Matched ftAnim functions retain address-based names such as ftAnim_8006E054 where semantic names are not yet known.
One public semantic name remains in use: ftAnim_GetNextJointInTree.
Fighter part enum/type names are used in signatures where known, for example Fighter_Part and FtPart_TransN.
Recovered animation data structures use conventional names FigaTree and FigaTrack instead of address-derived placeholder lbMthp_8001E8F8_t.
Unknown fighter fields remain offset-style names such as x594_b0, x8A4_animBlendFrames, x8AC_animSkeleton, and x590, but their types were improved where evidence allowed.
Forward-declarable structs are written as struct DObjList/FigaTree/FigaTrack in headers, with typedefs supplied from forward headers where needed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced static arrays ftAnim_804590D8, ftAnim_804590D8_unk, and ftAnim_804590D8_F0 as explicit traversal stacks for recursive-like joint iteration.
Used depth counters passed by pointer for AnimJoint, MatAnimJoint, and Joint traversal routines to mirror original state updates.
Used exact HSD_ForeachAnim argument constants such as JOBJ_TYPE, 0xFB7F, AOBJ_ARG_AU, AOBJ_ARG_AF, and flag 0x20000000.
Used separate no-blend and blend paths keyed on fp->x8A4_animBlendFrames == 0.0F.
Used bitfield flags such as x594_b0, x594_b1, x594_b2, x594_b3, x594_b4, x594_b5, x594_b6, x2221_b2, x2223_b0, and x2226_b2 to steer exact control flow.
Maintained original HSD_JObj flag checks such as !(HSD_JObjGetFlags(jobj) & 0x1000) during traversal.
Changed data symbol boundaries in symbols.txt so rodata/data references align with separate strings rather than a single oversized object.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased to 30.12%, +0.21%, +8236 bytes, with 30 new ftanim matches and ftCo_8009E4A8 improved to 100%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftAnim_8006E054, ftAnim_8006E7B8, ftAnim_8006E9B4, or ftAnim_8006EED4 to semantic names.
Look for follow-up work on ftAnim_80070308, which this PR improved to 80.95% but did not fully match according to the bot report.
Search for additional uses of FigaTree/FigaTrack in fighter data and dynamic bone code to confirm the recovered ftDynamics.x10 type.
Check later PRs for cleanup or semantic naming of Fighter fields x590, x594_bits, x597_bits, x8A4_animBlendFrames, and x8AC_animSkeleton.
Review future symbol updates around ftAnim_803C0670/803C0690/803C06A8 to see whether the string names were later given semantic labels.
